---
title: "SemesterView"
date: "2026-04-29"
categories: [Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, AI, Vercel]
---

## Overview

SemesterView is a semester-long calendar tool for college students that surfaces scheduling conflicts before they happen. Unlike week-by-week planners, it shows the entire semester at once — layering recurring commitments (classes, work shifts, meetings) on top of variable obligations (exams, deadlines, events) so students can spot collisions early.

Key features include:

- **Full-semester view** — see every week from first day of class to last final at a glance
- **Conflict detection** — automatically flags days with 3+ high-priority events or exact time overlaps
- **Recurring commitments** — add classes or shifts with days of week, time, and date range
- **Variable obligations** — track exams, deadlines, and personal events with categories
- **iCal import & live sync** — one-time import or live-syncing feed from Canvas, Google Calendar, or Learning Suite
- **AI Daily Assistant** — Claude-powered briefing that surfaces your most pressing priorities each morning
- **Click-to-add** — tap any time slot to pre-fill the event form

## Live Product Demo

<div class="d-flex justify-content-center gap-3 my-4">
  <a class="btn btn-primary d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://semesterview-app.vercel.app/"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open live app">
    <i class="fa-solid fa-window-restore me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    Open App in New Tab
  </a>

  <a class="btn btn-outline-dark d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://github.com/mnielsen705/semesterview-app"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open GitHub source">
    <i class="fa-brands fa-github me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    View Source on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

<iframe src="https://semesterview-app.vercel.app/"
        style="width:100%; height:900px; border:none;"
        allowfullscreen>
</iframe>

## Problem & Approach

College students with planning habits still drop the ball because their tools only show the near future. By the time a conflict becomes visible, it's already a crisis. SemesterView flips the model — it gives students the full-semester horizon so they can protect high-stakes weeks before they arrive.

The core design challenge was fitting an entire semester (16+ weeks) into a readable, mobile-friendly grid while still communicating event density and conflict severity at a glance.

## Technical Details

**Framework/Stack:** Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack), TypeScript

**UI Components:** shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, react-hook-form + Zod

**Backend:** Supabase (PostgreSQL + Row Level Security + Auth)

**AI:** Anthropic Claude (`claude-haiku-4-5`) via streaming API — daily briefing and follow-up Q&A

**Deployment:** Vercel (auto-deploy on push to main)

**Tools Used:**

- Claude Code for development assistance
- Supabase SSR for cookie-based session persistence
- Custom conflict detection engine (`lib/conflict-detection.ts`)
- iCal/RFC 5545 parser for Canvas and Google Calendar import
- Vercel Cron for daily live-sync feed refresh
